max_runtime: No fixed limit (starter quotas enforce TTLs; higher quota tiers unlock sandboxes with unlimited persistence)
name: Blaxel
notes: Blaxel offers $200 in free credits on signup and supports Zero Data Retention (ZDR) natively. The platform is CPU-only, with its own blog stating it is 'not suitable for GPU workloads.' Pricing is RAM-based rather than vCPU-based, which is unusual relative to most sandbox providers.
pricing_model: Per-second active compute billed by RAM-seconds (storage and snapshots billed separately per GB-month)
summary: Blaxel is a managed cloud platform that provisions one microVM per AI agent, app, or job so that LLM-generated code can execute in isolation. Each sandbox auto-suspends when idle and resumes in ~25 ms with both filesystem and in-memory process state preserved. It is used to host coding-agent harnesses such as the Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cline, and Cursor background agents.
